response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems developer with strong testing discipline and broad language exposure, but he falls critically short of the role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ programming experience — his C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor's thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. Similarly, 5 years of large-scale cloud infrastructure or compute platform work is required; his closest experience is application-layer high-throughput work at Dunnhumby and infrastructure-adjacent use of AWS CDK and Kubernetes at Tencent and in personal projects. The role demands a cloud platform technical lead, not a generalist web/backend engineer, making this a poor fit despite his seniority in other domains.
strengths: Strong engineering practices culture: demonstrated TDD advocacy, mentoring via workshops and code katas, and cross-team knowledge sharing across multiple roles.
Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O with DLang), showing some infrastructure-adjacent instincts.
Breadth of stack and tooling (TypeScript, Node.js, Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, CI/CD) indicates adaptability and willingness to own full delivery lifecycle.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — roughly 6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — against an 8-year minimum requirement; this is a disqualifying gap for a C++-centric platform role.

No demonstrated ownership of cloud compute infrastructure (VMs, networking, storage, hypervisors, or compute scheduling); experience is at the application and deployment tooling layer, not platform engineering.

No clear record of leading a technical team or setting architectural direction for infrastructure engineers; staff/senior titles reflect individual contribution rather than org-level technical leadership.
